description: Generate realistic, graph-connected mock data from a GraphQL schema using faker, plus a GraphQL Code Generator plugin that emits a SchemaTypeMap for typed mock pools.
rules: buildMocks(schema, options?) is the runtime entry point; it accepts a GraphQLSchema or an SDL string and returns a MockResult keyed by object-type name.
Relationships are wired as actual object references: todo.user is the same object as mocks.User[i], not a copy. Circular references resolve to already-generated objects.
Pools are unknown[] by default. Pass a TTypes map (buildMocks<{ User: User }>(schema)) or a generated SchemaTypeMap to get typed pools without a cast.
Use the @vantreeseba/graphql-mocks-codegen plugin to auto-generate the SchemaTypeMap from a schema; run it alongside the standard typescript plugin in codegen.ts.
Determinism comes from the seed option; override and scalar functions receive the generator's seeded faker instance, so do not import a separate faker for them.
Default counts are 5 per type; scalar list fields generate 1-3 items and relationship list fields 1-5. Unknown scalars fall back to faker.lorem.word() with a console.warn.

Every object gets __typename by default (Apollo cache needs it); disable with addTypename: false. stableIds gives id fields readable TypeName-<index> values, but an explicit overrides entry for id still wins.

ESM-only, TypeScript 5 strict. Peer deps: graphql >=16 and @faker-js/faker >=9 for the runtime; the codegen plugin also needs @graphql-codegen/plugin-helpers and @graphql-codegen/visitor-plugin-common.
